action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 2 (global Dijkstra, greedy utility/distance, no commitment) scored 0.6013 and remains the best. Iteration 3 (fixed target commitment) dropped to 0.577. Iteration 4 confirmed that passing non-neighbor targets to env.step causes erratic behavior, so we must step only along graph neighbors. Analysis: The biggest opportunities are (1) reduce oscillation/backtracking via history/momentum penalties and stronger visited discount, (2) better fallback when no node has utility>0 (navigate toward nearest frontier instead of random neighbor), and (3) discourage very long jumps with a larger distance denominator to improve path efficiency. Decision: Rewrite predict.py with an improved greedy heuristic: utility/(dist+3.6), visited penalty 0.05, history proximity penalty, momentum bonus, step-budget guard, and frontier-aware fallback. Run a quick smoke test, then submit eval to measure the new score and iteration time.
